entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The agricultural crop targeted for the management of stem rot disease.

entities.name: STEM ROT
entities.description: A fungal disease caused by Sclerotium rolfsii that affects groundnut seedlings.

entities.name: SCLEROTIUM ROLFSII
entities.description: The soil-borne fungal pathogen that causes stem rot in groundnut.

entities.name: PSEUDOMONAS FLUORESCENS
entities.description: A bacterial antagonist used for the biocontrol of Sclerotium rolfsii.

entities.name: TRICHODERMA VIRIDE
entities.description: A fungal antagonist that produces chitinase and parasitizes Sclerotium rolfsii.

entities.name: THIRAM
entities.description: A systemic fungicide used as a chemical control agent against stem rot.

entities.name: BIOCONTROL
entities.description: The use of biological agents like Pseudomonas fluorescens and Trichoderma viride to manage plant diseases.

entities.name: EXTRACELLULAR CHITINASE
entities.description: An enzyme produced by Trichoderma viride to degrade the mycelium of Sclerotium rolfsii.

entities.name: ANTAGONISTIC ACTIVITY
entities.description: The ability of biological isolates to inhibit the growth of a pathogen.

entities.name: CONTROLLED ENVIRONMENT CONDITIONS
entities.description: The experimental settings used to evaluate the efficacy of biocontrol agents and fungicides.

entities.name: MORTALITY
entities.description: The death rate of groundnut seedlings following inoculation with Sclerotium rolfsii.
